_comment: Pterodactyl Egg for Auralis — https://github.com/ybenyedder/auralis. Author: Youssef Ben yedder <volt@webtvmedia.net>
name: Auralis
author: volt@webtvmedia.net
description: Self-hosted, local-first music platform (Next.js). Scans a music folder, streams it, fetches lyrics and serves a web + native client UI. Builds from source on install and runs with `next start`.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
apt-get update
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git ca-certificates python3 make g++
mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server
# The install container runs as root but the files belong to the server user, so
# git would refuse with 'dubious ownership' — trust the build dir explicitly.
git config --global --add safe.directory '*'
if [ -d .git ]; then
  echo 'Updating existing checkout...'
  git fetch --all
  git reset --hard origin/${GIT_BRANCH:-main}
  git clean -fd -e music -e data
else
  echo 'Cloning Auralis...'
  git clone ${GIT_ADDRESS:-https://github.com/ybenyedder/auralis.git} .
  git checkout ${GIT_BRANCH:-main}
fi
echo 'Installing dependencies...'
# The desktop/mobile build tooling (Electron binary ~250MB, Puppeteer) is not
# needed to run the web server — skip those heavy downloads so the install fits.
export ELECTRON_SKIP_BINARY_DOWNLOAD=1
export PUPPETEER_SKIP_DOWNLOAD=1
export NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
npm ci --no-audit --no-fund
echo 'Building...'
npm run build
echo 'Trimming dev dependencies to shrink the runtime footprint...'
npm prune --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund || true
mkdir -p music data
# Optional: local forced-alignment (word-by-word karaoke). Installed only when the
# feature is enabled, and INTO data/ so it survives reinstalls (git clean above
# preserves music/ and data/). Never aborts the base install (|| true guards).
if [ "${AURALIS_LYRICS_FORCED_ALIGN}" = "true" ]; then
  echo 'Forced-align enabled — installing the local aligner (torch ~1.5GB + model ~1.2GB, into data/ so it survives reinstalls)...'
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3-pip python3-dev || true
  # Map the runtime data path (/home/container/...) to the install mount (/mnt/server/...).
  DATA_RT="${AURALIS_DATA_DIR:-/home/container/data}"
  case "$DATA_RT" in
    /home/container/*) DATA="/mnt/server/${DATA_RT#/home/container/}" ;;
    *) DATA="$DATA_RT" ;;
  esac
  DEPS="$DATA/pydeps"
  mkdir -p "$DEPS"
  # The runtime container's /tmp is a tiny tmpfs (~100MB) — point pip's temp at the
  # volume so the torch download/unpack does not fail with "No space left on device".
  export TMPDIR="$DATA/.tmp"; mkdir -p "$TMPDIR"
  if PYTHONPATH="$DEPS" python3 -c 'import torch, torchaudio' 2>/dev/null; then
    echo 'Aligner deps already present in data/pydeps — skipping torch download.'
  else
    python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --target "$DEPS" torch torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu \
      && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --target "$DEPS" numpy \
      || echo 'WARNING: aligner deps failed to install; forced align stays off until a successful reinstall.'
  fi
  # Pre-download the alignment model so the first on-demand run is fast (cached in data/).
  TORCH_HOME="$DATA/torch-cache" PYTHONPATH="$DEPS" python3 -c 'import torchaudio; torchaudio.pipelines.MMS_FA.get_model()' \
    || echo 'Model pre-download skipped (will fetch on first use).'
  rm -rf "$TMPDIR" 2>/dev/null || true
  # ffmpeg is already provided by the runtime image (yolks nodejs ships it) — no download needed.
fi
# Optional: deep audio embeddings (timbre/texture) for the recommender. Installed
# only when enabled, INTO data/pydeps so it survives reinstalls (git clean above
# preserves music/ and data/). Never aborts the base install (|| true / || echo guards).
if [ "${AURALIS_EMBEDDINGS}" = "1" ]; then
  echo 'Deep embeddings enabled — installing the extractor deps into data/pydeps...'
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3-pip python3-dev || true
  DATA_RT="${AURALIS_DATA_DIR:-/home/container/data}"
  case "$DATA_RT" in
    /home/container/*) DATA="/mnt/server/${DATA_RT#/home/container/}" ;;
    *) DATA="$DATA_RT" ;;
  esac
  DEPS="$DATA/pydeps"; mkdir -p "$DEPS"
  # The runtime container's /tmp is a tiny tmpfs (~100MB) — route pip's temp at the
  # volume so a wheel unpack does not fail with "No space left on device".
  export TMPDIR="$DATA/.tmp"; mkdir -p "$TMPDIR"
  if [ "${AURALIS_OPENL3}" = "1" ]; then
    echo '  backend: OpenL3 (tensorflow — large, ~1GB).'
    python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --target "$DEPS" numpy soundfile resampy openl3 tensorflow-cpu \
      || echo 'WARNING: OpenL3 deps failed to install; deep embeddings stay off until a successful reinstall.'
  else
    echo '  backend: librosa (light).'
    python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --target "$DEPS" numpy scipy soundfile librosa \
      || echo 'WARNING: librosa deps failed to install; deep embeddings stay off until a successful reinstall.'
  fi
  if [ "${AURALIS_STEMS}" = "1" ]; then
    echo '  stems: installing Demucs + torch (CPU) into data/pydeps...'
    python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --target "$DEPS" torch torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu \
      && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --target "$DEPS" demucs \
      || echo 'WARNING: Demucs deps failed to install; per-stem features stay off.'
  fi
  rm -rf "$TMPDIR" 2>/dev/null || true
fi
echo 'Auralis install complete. Upload your audio into the music/ folder.'

variables.name: Git Repository
variables.description: Source repository to clone and build.

variables.name: Music directory
variables.description: Folder scanned and streamed. Upload your audio here.

variables.name: Data directory
variables.description: Writable folder for the SQLite database, cover-art cache and session secret.

variables.name: Admin password
variables.description: Initial admin password. Leave blank to get a random one printed once to the console.

variables.name: Forced-align lyrics (word-by-word)
variables.description: Refine line-level lyrics into word-by-word karaoke locally by listening to the audio (no extra services). HEAVY: after setting this to true, press Reinstall — the install then puts ~1.5GB of ML deps (torch) into data/ (so they survive future reinstalls) and a ~1.2GB model is cached on first use. Needs at least ~4GB RAM. Leave false unless the server is beefy.

variables.name: Smart deep embeddings (timbre)
variables.description: Learn each track's TIMBRE/texture (not just energy/BPM) for sharper recommendations. HEAVY & opt-in: set to 1 then press Reinstall — the install puts the audio extractor's Python deps (librosa by default) into data/ so they survive reinstalls, and the extractor then runs in the background after each scan. Leave 0 on small servers; the recommender works great without it.

variables.name: Embeddings: use OpenL3 (advanced)
variables.description: Only read when Smart deep embeddings is on. 1 = use OpenL3 learned embeddings (installs tensorflow, ~1GB, best quality) instead of the default lightweight librosa embedding. Needs more RAM/disk. Do not switch backends on an already-embedded library. Press Reinstall after changing.

variables.name: Embeddings: Demucs stems (advanced)
variables.description: Only read when Smart deep embeddings is on. 1 = also run Demucs to summarise each track per instrument stem (vocals/bass/drums/other), appended to the embedding. VERY heavy (installs torch, wants ~6GB RAM). Press Reinstall after changing.
